meta.title: Joch — The control plane for AI agent fleets
meta.description: Joch is the vendor-neutral control plane for governing, securing, and operating AI agent fleets across every SDK, model, tool, and runtime.

hero.title: Harness your agent fleet.
hero.subtitle: Joch is the vendor-neutral control plane for securing, governing, and operating AI agents across every model, framework, tool, and runtime.

problem.kicker: The questions vendor SDKs leave to the operator
items: What agents exist across my company, and who owns them?
What models, tools, MCP servers, and memory does each agent depend on?
What did each agent do yesterday — and what did it cost?
Can I move an agent from OpenAI to Claude — and what would break?
Can I block all agents from calling email.send without approval?
Can I deploy the same agent locally, in Docker, and in Kubernetes?
Can I compare two agent versions before promoting to production?
Which MCP servers are pinned, and which were quarantined this week?
problem.footnote: If you operate ten, a hundred, or a thousand agents — you need a control plane.

pillars.title: Run agents like production infrastructure.
pillars.subtitle: Joch organizes everything you need to operate AI agents safely — from the inventory layer down to release management.

items.name: Inventory
items.summary: A single system of record for every agent, model, tool, MCP server, memory store, and owner — regardless of which SDK built them.

items.name: Governance
items.summary: Portable policy-as-code for tool use, data access, approvals, model choice, and budgets — enforced uniformly across SDKs.

items.name: Portability
items.summary: One agent record runs locally, in Docker, on Kubernetes, or via managed runtimes; conversation state migrates across providers.

items.name: Observability
items.summary: Every model call, tool call, memory write, RAG retrieval, and approval is captured as an event — exported to OpenTelemetry and OCSF.

items.name: Release Management
items.summary: Version, eval, diff, promote, and roll back agents like production software — with quality gates that block regressions.

products.title: One control plane. Six surfaces.
products.subtitle: Inspect, govern, distribute, and operate your fleet from open-source tools, hosted SaaS, or self-hosted enterprise.

items.name: Joch Console
items.summary: Open-source web console for inventory, traces, approvals, AgBOM, and cost views.

items.name: Joch Registry
items.summary: Open registry of reusable agent templates, policies, and tool / MCP definitions.

items.name: Joch Operator
items.summary: Kubernetes operator that reconciles agents, policies, and traces as first-class resources.

items.name: Joch Cloud
items.summary: Hosted multi-tenant control plane with SSO, RBAC, retention, and SLA — your runtime, our control plane.

items.name: Joch Enterprise
items.summary: Self-hosted enterprise distribution with airgap installer, hardened images, and FedRAMP-ready packaging.

items.name: Joch Marketplace
items.summary: Vetted MCP servers and tools with signing, security review, and SLAs.

integrations.kicker: Bring any agent. Run it anywhere.
integrations.title: Vendor-neutral by design.
integrations.subtitle: Joch sits above your SDKs and providers. Switch frameworks or models without rewriting governance.

items.name: Fleet Inventory
items.summary: Discover, register, and catalog every agent across SDKs and teams in one place.

items.name: MCP Governance
items.summary: Pin, scan, sandbox, and quarantine MCP servers across the entire fleet.

items.name: Cross-Provider Migration
items.summary: Move a live conversation between providers with a deterministic checkpoint and capability check.

items.name: Cost Control
items.summary: Cap, attribute, and alert on agent costs across teams and providers — without changing agent code.

items.name: Release Gates
items.summary: Block agent promotions on failing evals, AgBOM regressions, or missing approvals.

items.name: Approvals
items.summary: Route human approvals for risky tool calls without blocking the agent loop.

pricing.title: Open-core. Honest pricing.
pricing.subtitle: Pay for control-plane operations, never for inference. The OSS core is sufficient for production fleets.

tiers.name: Open Source
tiers.summary: Full control plane, gateways, AOS exporters, CLI, console — self-hostable end to end.

tiers.name: Cloud Starter
tiers.summary: Hosted control plane to remove the hosting friction. Limits on agents, executions, and retention.

tiers.name: Cloud Team
tiers.summary: The typical landing tier for engineering organizations.

tiers.name: Cloud Enterprise
tiers.summary: For regulated industries and large fleets where dedicated hosting matters.
features: Dedicated control plane in your cloud

pricing.footnoteBody: Joch Enterprise ships every Cloud feature in an airgap-friendly distribution with hardened images, FedRAMP packaging, and a dedicated CVE channel.

cta.title: Govern every agent. Audit every action.
cta.subtitle: Install Joch in front of your fleet. Discover, govern, trace, approve, deploy, roll back.
